fields.description:
## Why
Humans and a future CI gate need to see/consume all attestations on a ticket.
## What
Render the `attestations` map in `show` and the MCP ticket-state response (omitted when
empty). Document the model in `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (attestations section): when each
kind is emitted, additive-across/replace-within semantics, immutable-record +
`compute_validity` on read, the SNAPSHOT mirror + cutover, and how a future CI gate consumes
`verify-signature --kind K`. Document the HMAC symmetric-key constraint (a CI verifier needs
`REBAR_SIGNING_KEY`) as known follow-up; do NOT build a CI `gate-check` command here.
SCOPE BOUNDARY: this story owns the `attestations` field in the ticket-state MCP response;
the `verify_signature` MCP tool + its schema are owned by cc1b (rosy-vigor-yoke).
## Scope
`src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py` / show path, `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` (ticket-state
response only), `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, `CLAUDE.md` attestations note.
## Depends on
Requires 114f merged (state shape) and cc1b (verify surface) for consistent docs.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `show`/MCP render `attestations` (kind -> record); omitted when empty.
- [ ] `docs/plan-review-gate.md` documents emission per kind, semantics, `compute_validity`, SNAPSHOT mirror + cutover.
- [ ] CI-readiness (per-kind certified check) and the HMAC symmetric-key constraint documented as follow-up.
- [ ] No CI `gate-check` command added (scope note recorded).
